variant_description: Refrigerator assembly variant: two-sided line with 70+ stations, front/back task coordination, complex task precedence across sides. Based on the Egyptian refrigerator case study (context row 3). Serves Scenarios 1 and 2 for refrigeration products specifically. Target technology not yet committed.

Washing machine assembly variant: 100+ components, high-end configuration options, ~90% achievable balance rate. Based on the Midea case study (context row 2) — Midea used Visual Components simulation for this line, so visual_components is the known target technology. Serves Scenarios 1, 2, and 3 for washing machine lines.

Air conditioner assembly variant: 29-station commercial AC line, welding/gas-injection activities, 10-hour shifts. Based on the PMC air conditioner paper (context row 1). The paper itself uses decision tree ML rather than DES, so the PMC paper provides structural parameters but not a DES implementation — target technology for a DES variant is not yet committed. Serves Scenarios 1, 2, and 4 for AC products.

Generic home appliance line variant: teaching-oriented simplified variant suitable for introducing line balancing concepts without domain-specific complexity. Would serve Scenario 1 in a pedagogical context. Target technology not committed — natural candidates include simpy for accessible teaching or arena for classical manufacturing DES courses.
